_doc: CURRENT-DESIGN determinism baseline for details/platform.spec.yaml, re-frozen from the SPEC path at base SHA c4692f559fca83624064c52f04c5e51007845f3c by scripts/refreeze_from_spec.py (task STRUCT #19 / addendum A2). The imperative path is gone (milestone 4B); this corpus IS the canonical record the compiled spec must reproduce byte-for-byte. The imperative-era testimony is retrievable at b52626b. Regenerate only with a justified, reviewed diff: python scripts/refreeze_from_spec.py.

bom.assumptions: Actual dressed dimensions (PS 20). Long edges eased r=0.12"; end grain square.
Actual dressed dimensions (PS 20). Long edges eased r=0.12"; end grain square.
Thread represented as a revolved V-groove on the threaded zone; pitch exaggerated for legibility, not a fab spec.
5/4 decking dresses to 1" x 5.5"; modeled butt-tight, field-install with ~1/8" gaps. Long edges eased.
Actual dressed dimensions (PS 20). Long edges eased r=0.12"; end grain square.
Actual dressed dimensions (PS 20). Long edges eased r=0.12"; end grain square.

Exact prism as validation body; real precast pier block is tapered with a cast-in post saddle. Set on compacted grade (no separate leveling part modeled — the post's standoff is the leveling gap). P1: field-verify seating + bearing on grade.
Actual dressed dimensions (PS 20). Long edges eased r=0.12"; end grain square.
Actual dressed dimensions (PS 20). Long edges eased r=0.12"; end grain square.
Thread represented as a revolved V-groove on the threaded zone; pitch exaggerated for legibility, not a fab spec.
Mesh modeled as a single thin panel, not individual wires (envelope only); 2.00" openings satisfy the 4" sphere rule. Fastened with staples/screws + fender washers.
Actual dressed dimensions (PS 20). Long edges eased r=0.12"; end grain square.

Existing live tree — not cut, notched or purchased. Modeled as an exact cylinder (clean validation/ground body); the real trunk is irregular and grows, which the per-side growth gap (the beams clear the trunk, platform detail) accommodates. Bark and surface irregularity are field concerns, not modeled.
Exact prism as validation body; real boulder irregular — leveling nuts absorb surface variation. Render displacement is display-only.
Thread represented as a revolved V-groove on the threaded zone; pitch exaggerated for legibility, not a fab spec.

bom.dimensions: flat washer 0.82" OD x 0.38" ID x 0.08"

bom.assumptions: Finished hex nut; threads not modeled (bore at nominal).

Exact rectangular standoff as validation body; real connector is a formed galvanized saddle with a stand-off gap. Its concrete anchor and post fasteners are field hardware carried on this BOM line (installed per the manufacturer's schedule) — not modeled as separate parts in v1. P1: field-verify anchor embedment + fastener count.
